companies.decision_maker_title: CMO Alison Wagonfeld (new) / CEO Jensen Huang

signals.signal_label: First-Ever CMO Hired — Full Brand Review Underway
signals.description: NVIDIA appointed Alison Wagonfeld as its first-ever Chief Marketing Officer in January 2026, reporting directly to Jensen Huang. Wagonfeld is a 10-year Google Cloud marketing veteran who built Google Cloud from startup to a $60bn business. NVIDIA hiring their first CMO ever signals a fundamental shift from B2B chipmaker to consumer-facing AI brand. A new CMO's first 100 days = reviewing every partnership opportunity. NVIDIA has never done sports sponsorship — this is a completely greenfield opportunity with the world's most valuable company.
signals.news_headline: Nvidia Enlists Google Cloud Executive as Its Marketing Chief

signals.description: NVIDIA posted $68.1bn in Q4 revenue (up 73% YoY), closing fiscal 2026 at $215.9bn total revenue. They are flush with cash and looking to build brand awareness beyond the tech industry. The new CMO has an enormous budget to work with. When you have $215bn in revenue and have never done a sports partnership, the question isn't whether to start — it's where.
signals.news_headline: Nvidia smashes Q4 2026 with $68 billion in revenue

companies.decision_maker_title: CEO Josh D'Amaro (from March 18) / Chief Brand Officer

signals.signal_label: New CEO Takes Over THIS WEEK — March 18
signals.description: Josh D'Amaro becomes Disney CEO on March 18, 2026, replacing Bob Iger. D'Amaro is an experiences-first leader who grew Disney Experiences to $36bn in revenue and is the architect of Disney's $60bn global expansion including new international resorts (Abu Dhabi, etc.). New CEO = all strategic partnerships under review. D'Amaro's expansion strategy targets Asia and Middle East — exactly the markets where LFC has its strongest fanbase. ESPN is already in sports; D'Amaro could push Disney brands into football sponsorship territory.
signals.news_headline: Josh D'Amaro Named Next Chief Executive Officer of Disney

companies.decision_maker_title: CEO Kenta Kon (from April 1) / Global CMO

signals.signal_label: New CEO April 1 — Second Change in 3 Years
signals.description: Toyota's CFO Kenta Kon becomes CEO on April 1, 2026 — the second CEO change in three years, signalling urgency at the world's largest automaker. Toyota is fighting to stay relevant against EV competitors (Tesla, BYD) and needs to connect with younger, global consumers. A new CEO will reassess every brand partnership. Toyota has a fleet of 150 million vehicles globally but is losing brand momentum. LFC's young, global, digitally-engaged fanbase is exactly the audience Toyota needs to reach as they pivot to a mobility and EV future.
signals.news_headline: Toyota promotes finance chief Kenta Kon as CEO in second leadership change in 3 years

companies.decision_maker_title: CEO Enrique Lores (from March 1) / Chief Marketing Officer

signals.signal_label: New CEO Brought In to Fix the Brand — March 1
signals.description: PayPal appointed Enrique Lores as CEO effective March 1, 2026, replacing Alex Chriss after branded checkout performance deteriorated sharply in Q4. Lores previously led HP through a complete brand transformation. His explicit mandate is to reinvigorate the PayPal consumer brand and rebuild branded checkout. This is the definition of a company that needs visibility partnerships RIGHT NOW. PayPal has 400M+ active accounts globally, operates in every LFC market, and the new CEO is actively looking for ways to put the PayPal brand back in front of consumers.
signals.news_headline: PayPal Appoints Enrique Lores as Chief Executive Officer

companies.decision_maker_title: Chief Marketing Officer / Global Head of Partnerships

signals.signal_label: First-Ever FIFA Sponsorship — Football Budget Is Active
signals.description: Diageo signed as Official Spirits Supporter for the FIFA World Cup 2026 — the first spirits company ever to partner with FIFA. They're activating Johnnie Walker, Don Julio, Buchanan's, Casamigos, and Smirnoff across all 16 host cities. This tells you everything: Diageo has decided that football is their marketing channel of choice, they've committed significant budget, and they're building a football portfolio from scratch. As a UK-headquartered company already investing in global football, an LFC club partnership is the logical next asset to complete their football strategy — and the timing is perfect with the World Cup months away.
signals.news_headline: Diageo named Official Spirits Supporter for FIFA World Cup 26
